cards.back: The allocation of a set of available resources to a set of processes, either by priority or time slicing
The process of switching the CPU's attention from one process to another, involving saving and restoring the context of each process
A situation where two or more processes are unable to proceed because each is waiting for the other to release a resource
A graph used to model and analyze the allocation of resources in a system, showing the relationships between processes and resources
The different states that a process can be in, including Ready, Running, Waiting, Sleeping, Zombie, and Dead
